ddls:
CREATE TABLE "tweets" (
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tweet */,
  "text" TEXT /* Text content of the tweet */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who posted the tweet */,
  "time_posted" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the tweet was posted */,
  "retweets" INTEGER /* Number of retweets the tweet has received */,
  "impressions" INTEGER /* Total number of impressions for the tweet */,
  "engagement_rate" REAL /* Engagement rate of the tweet */,
  "sentiment_score" REAL /* Sentiment score of the tweet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweets_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., marketing staff, administrator) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "joined_date" TEXT /* Date the user joined the university */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tweet_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric entry */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet */,
  "likes" INTEGER /* Number of likes the tweet has received */,
  "shares" INTEGER /* Number of times the tweet has been shared */,
  "comments" INTEGER /* Number of comments on the tweet */,
  "engagement_rate" REAL /* Engagement rate of the tweet */,
  "sentiment_score" REAL /* Sentiment score of the tweet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweet_metrics_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "scheduled_tweets" (
  "scheduled_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each scheduled tweet */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet to be posted */,
  "scheduled_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the tweet is scheduled to be posted */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who scheduled the tweet */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the scheduled tweet (e.g., pending, posted, cancelled) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("scheduled_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_scheduled_tweets_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_scheduled_tweets_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_engagement" (
  "engagement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each engagement */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who engaged with the tweet */,
  "engagement_type" TEXT /* Type of engagement (e.g., retweet, reply, mention) */,
  "engagement_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the engagement occurred */,
  "engagement_text" TEXT /* Text content of the engagement */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("engagement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_engagement_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_engagement_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "content_categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each content category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the content category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the content category */,
  "parent_category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent category (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tweet_content" (
  "content_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each piece of content */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the content category */,
  "content_description" TEXT /* Description of the content */,
  "content_type" TEXT /* Type of content (e.g., image, video, text) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("content_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweet_content_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES content_categories ("category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweet_content_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "hashtags" (
  "hashtag_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each hashtag */,
  "hashtag_name" TEXT /* Name of the hashtag */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the hashtag */,
  "popularity_score" REAL /* Popularity score of the hashtag */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("hashtag_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tweet_hashtags" (
  "tweet_hashtag_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tweet-hashtag association */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet */,
  "hashtag_id" INTEGER /* ID of the hashtag */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_hashtag_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweet_hashtags_hashtag_id FOREIGN KEY ("hashtag_id") REFERENCES hashtags ("hashtag_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweet_hashtags_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "events" (
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each event */,
  "event_name" TEXT /* Name of the event */,
  "event_date" TEXT /* Date of the event */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the event */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the event */,
  "event_type" TEXT /* Type of event (e.g., conference, workshop, seminar) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "event_tweets" (
  "event_tweet_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tweet-event association */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* ID of the event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_event_tweets_event_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_id") REFERENCES events ("event_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_event_tweets_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "news_feed" (
  "news_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each news item */,
  "news_title" TEXT /* Title of the news item */,
  "news_date" TEXT /* Date of the news item */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the news item */,
  "author" TEXT /* Author of the news item */,
  "news_type" TEXT /* Type of news item (e.g., press release, article, blog post) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("news_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "news_tweets" (
  "news_tweet_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tweet-news item association */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet */,
  "news_id" INTEGER /* ID of the news item */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("news_tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_news_tweets_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_news_tweets_news_id FOREIGN KEY ("news_id") REFERENCES news_feed ("news_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "twitter_api_keys" (
  "api_key_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each API key */,
  "consumer_key" TEXT /* Consumer key for Twitter API authentication */,
  "consumer_secret" TEXT /* Consumer secret for Twitter API authentication */,
  "access_token" TEXT /* Access token for Twitter API authentication */,
  "access_token_secret" TEXT /* Access token secret for Twitter API authentication */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("api_key_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tweet_images" (
  "image_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each image */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet */,
  "image_url" TEXT /* URL of the image */,
  "image_description" TEXT /* Description of the image */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("image_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweet_images_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tweet_videos" (
  "video_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each video */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet */,
  "video_url" TEXT /* URL of the video */,
  "video_description" TEXT /* Description of the video */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("video_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweet_videos_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)
